feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced liquid houseplant or cactus fertilizer (e.g. dilute 10-10-10) monthly during spring-summer growing season only; do not feed in winter; heavy fertilization burns leaf margins

commonIssues: Root rot from overwatering is by far the most common problem — let the potting mix dry out completely between waterings and never leave the pot standing in water
Reduce watering significantly from fall to late winter (roughly monthly) while the plant is not actively growing
Avoid pouring water directly into the center of the leaf rosette — it can rot the crown
Toxic if ingested (saponins): vomiting, nausea, diarrhea in pets and humans; sap may irritate skin
Pests: mealybugs, spider mites, scale, thrips, vine weevil
Tall narrow habit can make the plant top-heavy — use a wide, heavy (e.g. clay/terracotta) pot for stability
Tender tropical (USDA zones 10-12): keep above ~10 C, protect from frost and cold drafts; grow as an indoor/container plant outside those zones

source.citation: Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Dracaena trifasciata: zones 10-12; well-draining potting mix; water regularly in the growing season with significantly reduced watering from fall to late winter; avoid pouring water on the rosette center; prefers warm bright locations but tolerates some shade, protect from hot afternoon sun; overwatering causes root rot; mealybugs and spider mites; wide clay pots used so the tall narrow plant does not topple; height 2-4 ft

North Carolina State Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox — Dracaena trifasciata: partial shade (direct sun only part of the day, 2-6 hours) with tolerance for very low light; zones 10a-12b; tolerates cool temperatures around 50 F (10 C); erect clumping broadleaf evergreen; allow soil to dry between waterings spring-autumn, water only monthly in winter; toxic if ingested (saponins); mealybugs, spider mites, scale, thrips

RHS — How to grow sansevierias (Growing Guide): water only after the compost has completely dried out, barely any water in winter; feed once a month April-September with liquid houseplant or cactus feed; ideal 15-24 C, keep warm in winter and avoid cold drafts; snug pots preferred, repot only when roots fill the container, into a pot only slightly larger; free-draining peat-free cactus compost or loam-based compost with one part grit; overwatering/root rot is the most damaging mistake; mealybugs, red spider mite, vine weevil

Clemson University HGIC — Dracaena factsheet (covers snake plant within Dracaena): feed with liquid foliage plant fertilizer once a month during spring and summer; heavy fertilization burns leaf margins; allow soil to dry between waterings then water thoroughly; normal house temperatures 60-70 F; overwatering causes root rot; scale and mites
RHS — How to grow sansevierias: snug container preference and repot-only-when-rootbound guidance back the small recommended container size
North Carolina State Extension + RHS — temperature bounds: NCSU 'tolerates cool temperatures around 50 F' sets coldHardyMinC=10; RHS ideal range 15-24 C; frost kills the plant outdoors below zone 10

Confidence note: baseIntervalDays=14 is a planner-derived growing-season interval from 'water only after the compost has completely dried out' (RHS) and 'allow soil to dry between waterings' (NCSU/MoBot) — no source states an exact day count; winter watering should stretch to ~30 days (dormancyMonths). hotWeatherMultiplier=0.8 is a conservative planner estimate

Confidence note: recommendedMinLiters=2 (~15 cm pot) and ideal 3-12 L are planner estimates from RHS snug-pot guidance and MoBot's wide-stable-pot note plus typical 2-4 ft mature size — no source states liters. heatStressC=35 is a conservative estimate; sources only give the 15-24 C ideal range. lightProfile hours map NCSU's 'partial shade, 2-6 hours direct sun' to target 4 / max 6; minSunHours=1 reflects documented tolerance of very low indoor light
